NCT02439749_ON.name: SPYRAL HTN-ON MED
NCT02439749_ON.group: On meds (SPYRAL HTN-ON medication-adherent stratum). Sister-stratum of SPYRAL HTN-OFF (NCT02439749_OFF) — same parent trial NCT02439749 with two protocol arms (medication-on vs medication-off). Different patient cohorts in each stratum (no overlap).

evidence.label: Office SBP change
evidence.text: RDN reduced office SBP by 7.0 mmHg vs sham at 6 months (Bayesian posterior probability of superiority >0.99).

evidence.label: Eligibility (AACT-derived)
evidence.text: AACT-derived (auto-populated 2026-05-04). Sex: ALL; min age 20 Years; max age 80 Years; healthy volunteers: f. evidence.label: Daytime Ambulatory SBP
evidence.text: Daytime ambulatory SBP was reduced by 6.3 mmHg more with ultrasound RDN vs sham (P=0.0001).

NCT02439749_OFF.name: SPYRAL HTN-OFF MED
NCT02439749_OFF.group: Off meds (SPYRAL HTN-OFF medication-naive stratum). Sister-stratum of SPYRAL HTN-ON (NCT02439749_ON). Distinct cohorts; meta-analysis can pool unless pooling depends on shared design effects.

evidence.label: 24h Ambulatory SBP
evidence.text: The 24h ambulatory SBP reduction was 3.9 mmHg greater with RDN vs sham (P<0.001).

evidence.text: 24h ambulatory SBP reduction was 6.6 mmHg greater with RDN vs sham procedure.

NCT01418261.name: SYMPLICITY HTN-3
NCT01418261.group: First-generation RDN (radiofrequency) vs sham — LANDMARK NEGATIVE pivotal

allOutcomes.title: Change in 6-month office SBP (primary, RDN vs sham; first-generation device, negative primary)

NCT01418261.snippet: Bhatt DL et al. NEJM 2014;370:1393-1401 (SYMPLICITY HTN-3). Largest sham-controlled RDN pivotal; NEGATIVE primary (did not meet superiority). Landmark finding that triggered second-generation device development.

evidence.label: Primary - 6-mo Office SBP
evidence.text: Office SBP change at 6 months: RDN -14.13 mmHg vs sham -11.74 mmHg; mean difference -2.39 mmHg (95% CI -6.89 to +2.12; P=0.26). Primary endpoint NOT MET. First-generation Medtronic Symplicity catheter (unipolar radiofrequency).

evidence.label: Scope note
evidence.text: SYMPLICITY HTN-3 used first-generation Medtronic Symplicity catheter; subsequent trials (SPYRAL, RADIANCE) used second-generation multi-electrode catheters. Inclusion in this NMA broadens scope beyond "modern second-generation devices" — declare pre-specified sensitivity split at GRADE.

evidence.label: Eligibility (AACT-derived)
evidence.text: AACT-derived (auto-populated 2026-05-04). Sex: ALL; min age 18 Years; max age 80 Years; healthy volunteers: f.
